channel.title: Sobolev-Spaces on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Sobolev-Spaces on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Distributions and Sobolev Spaces — Where Analysis Meets PDE
item.description:
The problem with classical derivatives#

Consider the PDE $-u'' = f$ on $[0,1]$ . If $f$ is continuous, classical solutions exist and are $C^2$ . But what if $f$ is an $L^2$ function — or worse, a point mass $\delta(x - x_0)$ ? The classical derivative doesn’t apply, yet physically meaningful solutions exist (a string with a point load develops a corner, not a discontinuity — it’s in $H^1$ but not $C^2$ ). We need a framework where “derivative” makes sense for non-smooth objects.
